title: Robinhood Reports Second Quarter 2024 Results
text: Robinhood reported strong Q2 2024 earnings with substantial year-over-year growth. Revenue: Total net revenues increased 40% year-over-year to $682 million. Transaction-Based Revenues: Increased 69% year-over-year to $327 million, primarily driven by options revenue of $182 million, up 43%, and cryptocurrencies revenue of $81 million, up 161%. Earnings Per Share: Net income increased year-over-year to $188 million, or diluted EPS of $0.21, compared to $25 million, or diluted EPS of $0.03, in Q2 2023. Other Key Metrics: Other revenues increased 19% year-over-year to $70 million, primarily due to increased Gold subscription revenues. Total operating expenses increased 6% year-over-year to $493 million, primarily due to increased marketing and growth investments. Funded Customers: Increased by 1.0 million year-over-year to 24.2 million. Investment Accounts increased by 1.4 million year-over-year to 24.8 million. Product Growth: Options trading and cryptocurrency volumes showed particularly strong demand during the quarter, highlighting the success of Robinhood's diversified trading platform.
